reasoning: The informal statement is a one-way implication: "if there exists a continuous section f with p∘f = id, then p is a quotient map." The corrected formalization uses → matching this directly. The original formalization uses ↔ (biconditional), which additionally asserts the reverse: "if p is a quotient map, then a continuous section exists." This reverse direction is false in general — for instance, the quotient map [0,1] → [0,1]/{0,1} ≅ S¹ is a quotient map but admits no continuous section (there is no continuous injection from S¹ into ℝ). Thus the original is logically stronger than the informal claim (the ← direction gives the informal claim, but the → direction adds an extra, false conclusion), making the original statement provably false. The error type is "adding_extra_conclusion" because an extra conclusion (the reverse implication) was appended.
